message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker

Update .beads/issues.jsonl reflecting current bead state after
cut-certified mobility implementation and new contract work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2525,7 +2525,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","title":"Create reusable dual-run test harness entrypoints and macros","description":"Provide the practical top-level API for writing differential tests so future pilot suites can focus on scenarios and invariants rather than harness ceremony.","design":"Build the ergonomic top-level harness entrypoints that pilot suites will actually call. These should hide the dual-run ceremony, make it obvious how to declare a scenario and its expectations, and consistently route failure information through the common artifact pipeline.

Decide whether helper macros, builders, or plain functions are the right fit based on how the existing test tree is structured. The key requirement is that future pilots are easy to read and hard to misuse. If adding a new differential suite feels like writing a tiny framework from scratch, this task is incomplete.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a reusable top-level harness API that pilot suites call directly.

The API makes scenario declaration and dual-run execution concise without hiding important semantics.

At least one focused unit-level consumer and one smoke-level e2e consumer use the same entrypoint shape, and the shared run metadata or logging surface is stable enough that operators can tell which scenario/profile/seed path actually ran.","notes":"This is the task that turns framework internals into something contributors will actually use.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:08.595344385Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:13:49.696551544Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T02:13:49.696521127Z","close_reason":"Implemented DualRunHarness builder with phase1(), from_identity(), lab(), live(), with_seed_plan(), run() API. DualRunResult with passed(), summary(). assert_dual_run_passes() convenience. 8 new harness tests (total 70 dual_run tests). Ergonomic entrypoint hides seed/metadata ceremony — pilots write lab and live closures only.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","ergonomics","harness","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:08.595344385Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:16.713713292Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:19.692629287Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:22.792267626Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","title":"Build harness contract unit suite, golden fixtures, and smoke e2e coverage","description":"Make the shared dual-run harness itself a strongly tested surface with unit coverage, golden fixtures, and a small end-to-end smoke path before pilots build on it.","design":"Build the harness-side verification suite that proves the shared scenario contract, helper layer, and public harness entrypoints behave correctly before any surface-specific pilot depends on them. This should include invalid-shape tests, seed/replay round-trips, golden fixtures for normalized scenario material, and ergonomic failure messages that make harness misuse obvious.

The suite should be deliberately fine-grained: unit tests for parser/validation behavior, golden fixtures for record shapes, and at least one small end-to-end smoke path proving the shared harness can execute a tiny dual-run case and emit understandable logs on failure.","acceptance_criteria":"The shared harness surface has comprehensive unit/contract coverage and golden fixtures.

Harness failures produce readable, detail-rich diagnostics instead of opaque assertion diffs.

At least one reusable smoke-level e2e exercise proves the shared harness can run a minimal dual-run case end-to-end.","notes":"This bead prevents the worst kind of debugging loop: not knowing whether a pilot failed because the runtime is wrong or because the common harness is quietly malformed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T20:18:44.000962525Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:26:59.996679841Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","golden","harness","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:26:48.138015880Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:18:44.000962525Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:26:51.108276971Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:26:54.066834558Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:26:57.032922294Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:26:59.996621953Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","title":"Integrate adversarial generation, DPOR exploration, and seed corpus management","description":"Turn existing fuzzing and schedule-exploration machinery into a source of replayable dual-run scenarios, with shrinkable seeds and corpus rules that make divergences cheap to reproduce and retain.","design":"This phase converts existing adversarial infrastructure into a durable source of dual-run test cases. The key idea is that fuzzing and DPOR should not stop at finding an interesting lab schedule; they should be able to emit replayable scenario descriptors, minimized seed material, and corpus entries that feed the differential comparator directly.

The generation path should preserve enough metadata to reproduce failures later, while remaining compact enough to keep and rotate in CI. The shrinker path should focus on semantic preservation: smaller scenarios that still trigger the same differential mismatch are more valuable than simply shorter traces.

This phase should also define seed and corpus lifecycle rules so divergence-causing cases graduate into permanent regression coverage rather than vanishing after one debug session.","acceptance_criteria":"Fuzzing and DPOR outputs can be promoted into replayable differential scenarios instead of staying lab-local curiosities.

The system can retain, shrink, and re-run mismatch-causing seeds deterministically enough to support debugging and regression prevention.

Promoted adversarial cases preserve enough lineage and labeling that later unit/e2e suites, reports, and artifact registries can explain where a scenario came from and why it matters.","notes":"Existing assets here include `src/lab/fuzz.rs`, `src/lab/explorer.rs`, and `tests/adversarial_witness_corpus.rs`. This phase should reuse those ideas rather than create a second unrelated corpus story.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":600,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:02.822611087Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:46:51.661764254Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adversarial","differential","dpor","epic","fuzzing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:02.822611087Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:02.124234197Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:05.136783411Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3cddg.12.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:08.112746263Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","title":"Promote FuzzHarness outputs into replayable ScenarioSpec cases","description":"Bridge lab fuzzing outputs into the shared scenario format so generated adversarial cases can graduate into deterministic dual-run regressions.","design":"Extend the existing fuzzing path so interesting outputs can be promoted into the shared scenario format instead of dying inside lab-local test code. That means preserving enough setup, perturbation, and seed information to reconstruct the case through the dual-run harness later.

The bridge should prefer semantically meaningful scenario material over raw internals. A replayable scenario is more valuable than a giant opaque dump. Future maintainers should be able to look at the promoted artifact and understand what adversarial shape it represents.","acceptance_criteria":"`FuzzHarness`-style generation can emit replayable scenario descriptors for the differential framework, and direct unit/contract coverage proves the promoted artifacts satisfy `ScenarioSpec` shape, seed serialization, and lineage rules.

Interesting lab-found cases are promotable into stable regression material instead of remaining transient fuzz outputs, with readable scenario identity plus structured provenance, artifact, and replay fields preserved.

At least one promoted fuzz case is exercised end-to-end through the shared replay/regression flow, and the resulting logs or summaries make it obvious how a fuzz discovery became durable differential coverage.","notes":"This task is about converting discovery into reusable evidence. If it only finds bugs once, it is not doing enough.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:14.481177620Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:06:40.461336475Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adversarial","differential","fuzzing","replay","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:25.798876847Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:28.755617087Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:14.481177620Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","title":"Promote FuzzHarness outputs into replayable ScenarioSpec cases","description":"Bridge lab fuzzing outputs into the shared scenario format so generated adversarial cases can graduate into deterministic dual-run regressions.","design":"Extend the existing fuzzing path so interesting outputs can be promoted into the shared scenario format instead of dying inside lab-local test code. That means preserving enough setup, perturbation, and seed information to reconstruct the case through the dual-run harness later.

The bridge should prefer semantically meaningful scenario material over raw internals. A replayable scenario is more valuable than a giant opaque dump. Future maintainers should be able to look at the promoted artifact and understand what adversarial shape it represents.","acceptance_criteria":"`FuzzHarness`-style generation can emit replayable scenario descriptors for the differential framework, and direct unit/contract coverage proves the promoted artifacts satisfy `ScenarioSpec` shape, seed serialization, and lineage rules.

Interesting lab-found cases are promotable into stable regression material instead of remaining transient fuzz outputs, with readable scenario identity plus structured provenance, artifact, and replay fields preserved.

At least one promoted fuzz case is exercised end-to-end through the shared replay/regression flow, and the resulting logs or summaries make it obvious how a fuzz discovery became durable differential coverage.","notes":"This task is about converting discovery into reusable evidence. If it only finds bugs once, it is not doing enough.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:14.481177620Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:50:07.148973418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adversarial","differential","fuzzing","replay","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:25.798876847Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:28.755617087Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:14.481177620Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","title":"Promote DPOR and schedule exploration outputs into minimized differential scenarios","description":"Make explored schedules first-class differential inputs by preserving the schedule constraints that matter and minimizing them into durable reproducer material.","design":"Extend the DPOR and schedule-exploration path so explored interleavings can be represented as compact scenario/schedule material for the differential framework. The emphasis should be on preserving the semantic schedule constraints that matter, not encoding every incidental detail of one particular execution.

Minimization belongs here too. A good promoted schedule is small enough to reason about, still triggers the same mismatch, and can be fed back into debugging or CI without carrying a huge irrelevant trace burden.","acceptance_criteria":"DPOR or similar schedule exploration can produce replayable differential scenarios or schedule hints, and direct unit/contract coverage proves the promotion path preserves the schedule constraints that actually matter semantically.

The promoted artifacts are minimized enough to be useful in debugging and regression suites, with shrink lineage and retained evidence making clear what corner condition survived minimization.

At least one promoted schedule-derived case is shown end-to-end through the shared replay/report flow, with structured logs, artifact pointers, and reproduction instructions preserving both the original and minimized lineage.","notes":"This task turns schedule exploration from a lab-only investigation tool into a source of durable cross-runtime test inputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:20.347589283Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:06:57.687757904Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","dpor","replay","schedules","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:31.674448622Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:34.677039510Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:20.347589283Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","title":"Define divergence corpus registry, shrinker rules, and retention metadata","description":"Create the long-lived storage and lifecycle rules for mismatch-causing seeds so discoveries survive debugging and become durable regression coverage when appropriate.","design":"Define how mismatch-causing scenarios are named, stored, shrunk, and retained over time. This includes metadata such as originating surface, seed lineage, shrink status, first-seen context, and whether the case is now a permanent regression or still under investigation.

The retention rules should support both debugging and long-term project hygiene. The corpus should grow by admitting high-value seeds, not by hoarding opaque one-offs forever. Shrinking should preserve semantic mismatch class, not just reduce byte size or step count mechanically.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a documented registry format and lifecycle for divergence-causing seeds and scenarios.

Registry metadata records at least surface/profile context, divergence class, first-seen lineage, minimization lineage, artifact pointers, and regression-promotion state.

The registry rules are exercised by direct validation tests and by at least one end-to-end promotion path so future contributors can add new mismatch artifacts without improvising storage conventions or losing provenance needed for debugging, reporting, or replay.","notes":"A corpus without lifecycle rules becomes a junk drawer. This task keeps the artifact story sustainable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:26.265634606Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:51:47.399409203Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","differential","regression","shrink","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:43.539868544Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:26.265634606Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:37.607638857Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:40.554822795Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","title":"Capture live-runtime semantic evidence and normalize it for comparison","description":"Build the live-side adapter, instrumentation, and normalization pipeline needed to compare real executions against lab expectations without depending on fragile raw event-order equality, and verify that translation layer directly with deterministic fixtures and mismatch diagnostics.","design":"This phase makes live execution observable enough to compare meaningfully. The requirement is not perfect raw trace identity; it is sufficient semantic evidence to answer the comparator questions defined in the contract phase.

Live-side collection should prefer stable semantic markers over brittle internal details. The runtime adapter should aim for execution paths that reduce gratuitous scheduler noise, such as current-thread setups where appropriate, while still exercising the actual live code paths under test.

Normalization is a first-class design concern here. The output should collapse lab and live evidence into the same semantic record type so the differential engine is comparing comparable facts instead of raw heterogeneous traces.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a live-runtime adapter that can execute differential scenarios and emit semantic evidence suitable for comparison.

Normalization rules exist for the main observables under test and avoid depending on unstable raw ordering where ordering is not part of the contract.

The live capture and normalization path has dedicated unit/fixture coverage, representative golden records, and field-level mismatch diagnostics rather than being tested only transitively through later pilots.","notes":"This phase should reuse test setup and runtime-builder patterns already present in `tests/common/mod.rs` and related runtime tests. It is intentionally separate from the comparator phase because evidence collection and mismatch judgment are different responsibilities.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:05.880143236Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:01:52.000549348Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","epic","evidence","live","trace","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:05.880143236Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T21:01:52.000491300Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:11.051481361Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:14.016051971Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -5316,7 +5316,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.2","title":"[WASM][SharedWorker] Implement bootstrap, version handshake, and fallback to dedicated/inline lanes","description":"Implement the SharedWorker bootstrap and fallback plumbing. The browser SDK and runtime should be able to negotiate a SharedWorker coordinator when supported, perform version/feature handshake, attach per-client ports, and automatically demote to dedicated-worker or inline execution if the coordinator is missing or unhealthy.","design":"Build the concrete bootstrap path: capability detection, worker script provisioning, version handshake, client registration, health signaling, and downgrade on any non-truthful state. Reuse the execution-ladder policy and diagnostics model so SharedWorker selection looks like a lane upgrade, not a separate product with different failure semantics.

Implementation must include unit coverage for version negotiation and coordinator-state transitions, plus browser-run multi-tab or multi-context scripts that exercise attach, coordinator reuse, version mismatch, and downgrade while capturing structured topology logs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Browser Edition can auto-negotiate a SharedWorker coordinator when the host supports it and policy allows it.
2. Failure to attach, bootstrap, or remain healthy demotes automatically to a lower truthful lane.
3. The implementation reuses existing diagnostics and lane-selection primitives rather than inventing a parallel control plane.
4. Unit tests and browser-run multi-context scripts prove that SharedWorker bootstrap, downgrade, and emitted logs remain aligned.","notes":"The implementation bead is successful only if callers can treat SharedWorker as an optional optimization and future maintainers can rerun the same attach or downgrade scenarios with preserved logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:11:00.391508143Z","created_by":"SageOwl","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:53:24.964148073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","fallback","implementation","sharedworker","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:24:52.800307694Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:11:00.391508143Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:24:35.979790590Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.3","title":"[WASM][SharedWorker] Prove multi-tab reuse, crash recovery, and semantic equivalence","description":"Prove that SharedWorker improves topology without changing semantics. This bead adds tests and artifacts for multi-tab reuse, client churn, coordinator crash/restart, version mismatch, and lane demotion so the fallback promise remains executable evidence.","design":"Create matrix coverage that compares shared-worker, dedicated-worker, and inline lanes under both success and failure conditions. Verify that results, cancellation/drain behavior, and ownership boundaries remain equivalent while diagnostics correctly record the selected or demoted lane. Include explicit negative tests for browsers without SharedWorker or with incompatible version scopes.

The bead must ship both focused unit tests for pure coordinator or topology logic and browser-harness or e2e scripts for true multi-tab reuse, crash recovery, and reconnect behavior, each with detailed logging and stable repro commands.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Automated coverage exists for SharedWorker success, downgrade, version mismatch, coordinator crash, and client churn.
2. The test matrix proves semantic equivalence with lower lanes while allowing topology-specific diagnostics to differ.
3. Release evidence can explain why SharedWorker is enabled, skipped, or demoted in a given environment.
4. Unit and browser-run evidence emit detailed topology and downgrade logs that future maintainers can compare across runs.","notes":"Without this bead, SharedWorker support would be a claim about convenience rather than a demonstrated correctness-preserving feature with reproducible diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:11:41.348006024Z","created_by":"SageOwl","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:53:41.691376934Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","sharedworker","testing","validation","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:26:25.591343697Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:11:41.348006024Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:25:18.919500529Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","title":"[WASM][ServiceWorker] Productize service-worker support as a bounded broker/orchestration surface","description":"Productize service-worker support only as a bounded broker and orchestration surface. Service workers can materially improve resilience for fetch mediation, durable handoff, wake-triggered coordination, and artifact sync, but their lifecycle is too preemptible to pretend they are a normal always-alive Asupersync runtime. This branch captures the useful browser-native value while preserving the truth that ordinary application execution must still fall back to dedicated-worker or inline lanes when a service-worker broker is absent or unsuitable.","design":"Model the service worker as a broker that owns explicit durable handoff and coordination responsibilities, not arbitrary long-lived task execution. Define which operations may traverse the broker boundary, how durable state in IndexedDB-class storage supports restart reconciliation, how fetch/background triggers map into explicit capabilities, and when the runtime must demote back to lower lanes. Keep the non-goal of "general full-service-worker runtime parity" explicit in docs, diagnostics, and tests.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Browser Edition has a truthful service-worker plan centered on bounded brokering rather than fake general-runtime support.
2. The plan explains how service-worker-triggered work reconciles with durable storage and lower-lane fallback.
3. Restart, eviction, or absence of a service worker cannot violate ownership, cancellation, or artifact integrity guarantees.","notes":"This branch turns a tempting but misleading concept into a disciplined feature: service workers are useful, but only within a bounded contract that acknowledges their host lifecycle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:14:24.328769684Z","created_by":"SageOwl","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:17:57.992809286Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","fallback","serviceworker","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:17:57.992749365Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3ak5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:17:22.764902416Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:14:24.328769684Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.1","title":"[WASM][ServiceWorker] Define bounded broker model, persistence contract, and explicit non-goals","description":"Define the bounded broker contract, persistence assumptions, and explicit non-goals for service-worker support. This bead should make it impossible for later work to quietly expand from fetch/background brokering into unsupported claims about always-alive runtime execution.","design":"Specify which brokered operations are allowed, which durable data must survive worker termination, how capabilities are re-established on restart, and where the boundary with inline or dedicated-worker execution lies. Include explicit non-goals such as pretending that arbitrary application scopes may live indefinitely inside the service worker or that unsupported browser hosts should receive partial success. Tie the contract to the existing durable-storage artifacts so restart reconciliation has a concrete substrate.","acceptance_criteria":"1. There is one authoritative contract for service-worker broker responsibilities, durable state, and downgrade reasons.
2. The contract names explicit non-goals so future work cannot blur bounded brokering into fake runtime parity.
3. Durable restart reconciliation requirements are concrete enough to test mechanically.","notes":"This bead protects the project from overselling service workers while still making the useful browser-native pattern shippable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:14:57.788069464Z","created_by":"SageOwl","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:26:42.339122975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","contract","policy","serviceworker","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:26:42.339055900Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3ak5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:18:33.220163850Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:14:57.788069464Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.2","title":"[WASM][ServiceWorker] Implement broker registration, durable handoff, and fallback integration","description":"Implement broker registration, durable handoff, and fallback integration for the service-worker surface. The browser-facing layer should be able to detect a service-worker broker, register the supported contract, persist any required handoff metadata, and fall back cleanly when the broker is missing, stale, incompatible, or denied by policy.","design":"Build the concrete browser/package/runtime path for service-worker registration, capability advertisement, durable broker metadata, and controlled handoff into lower lanes when brokering is not viable. Reuse the execution-ladder diagnostics and policy reason codes so service-worker support reads like a bounded optional host lane rather than an unrelated one-off feature.

Implementation must include unit coverage for broker registration state, durable handoff metadata, and downgrade decision points, plus browser-run service-worker scripts that exercise registration, stale broker detection, restart, and lower-lane fallback while capturing durable resume logs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Supported brokered operations can register through a service worker with durable handoff metadata.
2. Missing, stale, or incompatible brokers demote cleanly without throwing away the baseline runtime.
3. The implementation keeps the bounded broker model visible instead of silently expanding scope.
4. Unit tests and browser-run service-worker lifecycle scripts prove that registration, handoff, and logged downgrade behavior stay aligned.","notes":"The implementation is only correct if it is obviously impossible to confuse this broker surface with a claim of full service-worker runtime parity, and if its lifecycle can be rerun from preserved scripts and logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:15:31.239368567Z","created_by":"SageOwl","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:54:35.075226223Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","fallback","implementation","serviceworker","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:27:15.971650862Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:15:31.239368567Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:26:59.151990638Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.3","title":"[WASM][ServiceWorker] Prove terminate/restart reconciliation and no-leak fallback behavior","description":"Prove terminate/restart reconciliation and no-leak fallback behavior for the service-worker broker. This bead adds deterministic and browser-harness evidence for broker absence, eviction, restart, version drift, and durable resume so the branch remains self-documenting and trustworthy.","design":"Construct a matrix that exercises normal brokered operation, broker eviction mid-flow, restart with durable handoff, version mismatch, and complete broker absence. Verify that observable semantics remain correct, durable state is neither lost nor duplicated, and diagnostics explain each downgrade or replayed handoff.

The matrix must combine focused unit tests for handoff and replay logic with browser-harness or e2e scripts that drive real service-worker termination, restart, and resume paths while capturing durable state and support logs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Automated coverage exists for service-worker success, absence, eviction, restart, and downgrade paths.
2. Restart reconciliation proves durable handoff is neither lossy nor duplicate-producing.
3. Release artifacts can explain when service-worker brokering is available, skipped, or intentionally disabled.
4. Unit and browser-run evidence emit detailed lifecycle, handoff, and downgrade logs that future maintainers can compare across runs.","notes":"This is the evidence bead that keeps the service-worker story honest under the exact lifecycle hostility that makes the host tricky, and it must leave behind scripts and logs that make debugging practical.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:16:12.293023483Z","created_by":"SageOwl","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:59:45.802034626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","serviceworker","testing","validation","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:27:49.598070002Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:59:45.801960488Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:16:12.293023483Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:27:32.784929778Z","created_by":"SageOwl","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
